rationale: This page contains detailed information on Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy (MRT), the definition and classification of stem cells (ESCs, iPSCs, ASCs), and news regarding the Animal Stem Cell Biobank and Phenome India National Biobank. These are core topics in biotechnology and health.
cleanedText:
• In MRT an embryo contains:
o Nuclear DNA from a man and a woman (the prospective mother)
o Mitochondria in an egg donated by another woman (the mitochondrial donor).
• Since, the child born will have three distinct genetic materials, it is called a Three-Parent Baby.
• Techniques: MRT include different techniques like Maternal Spindles Transfer (ST) and Pronuclear Transfer (PNT).
o PNT is done after fertilization whereas MST is done before fertilization.
1.4. STEM CELLS
Why in the News?
Recently, a study has found that stem cells extracted from adipose tissue (body fat) can aid in the healing of osteoporosis-related fractures in humans.
More on the News
• Osteoporosis is a disease which makes bones weak and fragile.
About Stem Cells
• Meaning: They are unspecialized cells that can self-renew indefinitely and can also differentiate into more mature cells with specialized functions.
o In humans, they have been identified in inner cell mass of the early embryo; in some tissues of foetus, umbilical cord, placenta; and in several adult organs.
• Key Types: Pluripotent stem cells (embryonic stem cells and induced pluripotent stem cells) and Nonembryonic/Somatic/Adult stem cells.
o Pluripotent stem cells: Ability to differentiate into all cells of the adult body.
Embryonic Stem Cells (ESCs): Found in the embryo (3-5 days post fertilization and prior to implantation) containing an inner cell mass capable of generating to all the specialized tissues of human body.
Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs): Created through the introduction of embryonic genes into a somatic cell (For E.g. Skin Cell) causing it to revert back to a “stem cell like” state.
o Adult Stem Cells (ASCs): Undifferentiated cells found in tissue or organ and can differentiate to yield the specialized cell types of that tissue or organ.
Key Types Include: Neural Stem Cells; Skin Stem Cells, etc.
Related News
Animal Stem Cell Biobank
• India’s first state-of-the-art animal stem cell biobank and laboratory inaugurated at National Institute of Animal Biotechnology (NIAB), Hyderabad.
• About Animal Stem Cell Biobank
o Key Features: Stem cell culture unit, 3D bioprinter, bacterial culture lab, cryostorage, etc.
o Significance: Focus on regenerative medicine and cellular therapies for livestock; advance research in disease modelling, tissue engineering, etc.
o Supported by: National Biopharma Mission (NBM) of Department of Biotechnology – BIRAC.
NBM is an industry-Academia Collaborative Mission for accelerating biopharmaceutical development.
1.5. PHENOME INDIA NATIONAL BIOBANK
Why in the News?
National Biobank inaugurated at the CSIR-Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology (IGIB).
